channel.title: 12V Off-Grid Power System for Shipping Container on CasaClawd Research
channel.description: Recent content in 12V Off-Grid Power System for Shipping Container on CasaClawd Research

item.title: Executive Summary
item.description:
Bottom Line

A 12V off-grid solar system on your Killarney container is very doable and well-suited to the climate. Using secondhand panels from Gumtree/Facebook Marketplace can cut costs significantly. For lights + fridge, budget AUD$1,800-2,400 for a reliable LiFePO4 system, or AUD$1,400-1,800 with lead-acid (shorter lifespan, more maintenance).

Key Findings

  1. Killarney sun hours are good: 4.5-5.5 peak sun hours, but winter performance drops ~30%
  2. Frost risk matters: LiFePO4 batteries can’t charge below 0°C—need heating or insulation in winter
  3. Panel mounting is solved: Domino Clamps ship to Australia (4-6 days), no drilling required
  4. 12V fridges are efficient: Modern compressor fridges use 40-80W, ~1-1.5kWh/day—very manageable
  5. Secondhand panels viable in QLD: Gumtree/FB Marketplace have regular listings at $50-100 per 250-300W panel
  6. MPPT worth it: 5-30% more power, especially important in suboptimal winter conditions

Recommendation

Go with LiFePO4 + MPPT despite higher upfront cost. Killarney’s frost risk means you need low-temperature protection (many LiFePO4 models have this built-in). Source panels secondhand from Brisbane/Gold Coast listings. Use Domino Clamps for no-drill mounting.

item.title: Constraints & Requirements
item.description:
The Problem

Power a shipping container at Killarney (Mum’s property) with 12V DC for:

  • LED lighting (interior/exterior)
  • 12V refrigerator (continuous load)
  • Possibly phone charging, small devices

Container is ~75m from main house. Using secondhand solar panels to minimize cost.

Location-Specific Constraints (Killarney)

Constraint Value Notes
Elevation ~700m Cooler temps, frost risk in winter
Peak sun hours 4.5-5.5 hrs/day Good, but seasonal variation
Winter performance -30% vs summer Shorter days, lower sun angle
Frost days 30-50/year LiFePO4 charging issues
Distance to house ~75m Separate system justified
Grid access Town power available But container is off-grid by location

Hard Constraints

Constraint Value Notes
Mounting surface Shipping container roof ~2.4m x 6m (assuming 20ft)
Panel source Secondhand/Gumtree/FB Marketplace Must verify condition before purchase
System voltage 12V DC Matches fridge and lighting; simpler wiring
Weather exposure Full outdoor Waterproofing, wind load, hail risk
Container integrity No drilling preferred Avoid roof penetrations if possible
Time available Weekends only, sporadic Project needs to be modular/pausable

Power Requirements (Estimated)

Load Watts Hours/Day Wh/Day
12V Fridge 50W avg 24 1,200
LED lights (4x) 20W total 4 80
Phone charging 10W 2 20
Total Daily ~1,300 Wh

With 20% buffer + Killarney winter derating: ~2,000 Wh/day target

item.title: Research Findings
item.description:
Source 1: Renogy AU - 200Ah LiFePO4 Battery

URL: https://au.renogy.com/products/deep-cycle-batteries/lithium-batteries/

Credibility: High — Major brand, AU store, local warranty

Key points:

  • 12.8V 200Ah LiFePO4: AUD$729-899 (on sale)
  • Low-temperature protection model available (critical for Killarney)
  • 5-year warranty
  • Free shipping Australia-wide

Notes: Price is competitive with imports when you factor in warranty and local support. Low-temp model is essential for Killarney winters.

Source 2: LiTime AU - 200Ah LiFePO4

URL: https://au.litime.com/products/litime-12v-200ah-plus-lifepo4-lithium-battery

Credibility: High — Popular budget brand, AU store

item.title: Options Comparison
item.description:
System Architecture Options

Option Pros Cons Est. Cost (AUD) Best For
Budget Lead-Acid Lowest upfront, proven tech Heavy, short lifespan, 50% usable, maintenance $1,400-1,800 Occasional use, tight budget
Mid-Range LiFePO4 Good lifespan, lightweight, low-temp protection Higher upfront $1,800-2,400 Daily use, Killarney frost
Premium Victron 10+ year life, best MPPT, expandability Expensive $2,400-2,800 Set-and-forget, expansion ready

Deep Dive: Budget Lead-Acid System

Components

  • Panels: 2x 300W secondhand ($100-150 each from Gumtree)
  • Controller: 30A PWM ($50-80) or budget MPPT ($120)
  • Batteries: 2x 100Ah AGM lead-acid ($200-250 each)
  • Mounting: Domino Clamps 4x ($200-250 shipped)
  • Wiring/fuses: $100-150
  • Fridge: Alpicool C20 ($300-400)

Total: ~AUD$1,400-1,800

Why it works

  • Lowest barrier to entry
  • AGM batteries maintenance-free
  • Easy to find locally (Battery World, etc.)

Why it might not

  • Batteries last 2-3 years with daily cycling
  • 50% DoD = only 100Ah usable from 200Ah bank
  • Heavy (60+ kg for battery bank)
  • Frost risk: Lead-acid handles cold better, but still derated
  • Voltage sag under load affects fridge

Deep Dive: Mid-Range LiFePO4 System (Recommended)

Components

  • Panels: 2x 300W secondhand ($200-300 from Gumtree/FB)
  • Controller: Renogy Rover 30A MPPT ($135-180)
  • Battery: Renogy 200Ah LiFePO4 with low-temp protection ($730-900)
  • Mounting: Domino Clamps + brackets ($200-280 shipped)
  • Wiring/fuses/breakers: $150-200
  • Fridge: Alpicool C20 or AD40 ($350-500)

Total: ~AUD$1,800-2,400

Why it works

  • 200Ah @ 80% DoD = 160Ah usable (vs 100Ah lead-acid)
  • 5-10 year lifespan
  • Lightweight (~25kg vs 60kg)
  • Low-temp protection built-in (critical for Killarney)
  • Flat discharge curve = stable voltage for fridge
  • MPPT extracts more from secondhand panels
  • Bluetooth monitoring (check from house)

Decision Matrix

Score 1-5 for each criterion:

item.title: Sources & References
item.description:
Primary Sources (Australian)

  1. Renogy AU Official — Batteries, controllers, AU warranty
  2. LiTime AU — Budget LiFePO4 batteries
  3. Gumtree Brisbane - Used Panels — Secondhand panel marketplace
  4. Domino Clamps Official — Container mounting, ships to AU
  5. Solar Online Australia — Victron, MPPT controllers
  6. Solar 4 RVs — AU solar specialist, advice
  7. Pack & Pitch - Camping Fridges AU — AU-focused fridge reviews

Secondary Sources

  1. The Camping Nerd - Fridge Comparison — Independent testing
  2. Reddit r/vandwellers - Fridge Advice — Real user experiences
  3. Overland Bound - 12V Fridge Thread — Community recommendations

Climate Data

  • BOM Killarney climate — Frost days, sun hours
  • Killarney elevation: ~700m (affects temperature and sun exposure)

Local Suppliers (Warwick/Toowoomba/Brisbane)

  • Battery World — Lead-acid, some lithium (Toowoomba)
  • Jaycar — Wiring, fuses, basic components (Warwick/Toowoomba)
  • Bunnings — General hardware, Unistrut, conduit
  • Solar 4 RVs — Online, ships AU-wide, expert advice

Tools Used

  • Date researched: 2026-03-08
  • Currency: AUD$
  • Location context: Killarney, QLD (rural, elevated, frost risk)
